purpose: The commit is adding a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue function to return an error if HCI_UNREGISTER flag has been set. This flag is set when hci_unregister_dev is called which could lead to use-after-free (UAF) errors.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c. This is a perfective commit because it improves the code quality by removing unnecessary includes.
The commit updates documentation for a store metric named 'N/A'. This is an addition of a new metric to the report table. The purpose of this change is to reflect changes in the report table.

The commit fixes a bug in hugetlb handling by introducing a vma lock for sharing huge pages. It also prevents bugs related to unmapping of huge page ranges and improves the code quality by adding comments and improving the readability.

The commit adds two new properties to a YAML file related to the Realtek rt5682s codec. These properties are AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply which represent the regulators supplying power for analog signals and microphone bias respectively.

The commit fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one(). The error handling path of the probe releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function. In some cases, an ioremap() must be undone. This is done by adding an iounmap() call in the remove function.
The commit removes an unused field 'work' from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in file ktls_tx.c.
The commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed in cfg80211.h file. This change is likely related to handling of key indices which can be negative or null.
The commit is modifying two defconfig files in the RISC-V architecture. It removes a specific configuration option for surface platforms from these defconfigs.

The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() and replaces an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode with this new function. This is done in multiple files where the code checks if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.

The commit is about skipping tests when a Real Time Clock (RTC) device is not present. It modifies the rtctest.c file by adding an if condition to check if the RTC device exists before performing operations on it.
The commit is a refactoring that removes an unnecessary assignment to variable 'bytes'. The commit cleans up a clang scan build warning.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks in a kernel configuration file. It also modifies the source code to handle this decoupling.
The commit is a refactoring in the file 'arch/x86/kvm/mmu/spte.h'. It removes duplicated 'be split' in the comment.

The commit reverts a change that was made to populate a clock table based on DCFCLK. The purpose of this change is to prevent an underflow situation when DCFCLK states are disabled and the PMFW fills out the table using DCFCLK values.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (a.k.a R9A07G054) to the Mali-Bifrost GPU in the devicetree bindings.

The commit changes various files related to PCIe drivers in Linux kernel. It appears to be a refactoring effort to replace the old 'pcie_port' structure with the new 'dw_pcie_rp' structure. This change is likely part of an ongoing effort to modernize and streamline the design of these drivers.

The commit fixes a clang warning about format specifier mismatch. The warning is related to printf-style functions where variadic arguments undergo default argument promotion. In this case, the 'h_proto' member of the 'eth' struct is an unsigned short (u16), but it gets promoted to int in the printf function call. The commit changes the format specifier from '%#06hx' to '%#06x', which matches the type of 'h_proto'. This change fixes the warning and makes the code more accurate.

The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture code related to jump labels. The bug is about checking if the branch offset fits within the instruction's bit field. The commit changes the upper bound of the branch range to long to do signed comparison, avoiding negative offset triggering this warning.
The commit removes a hardware variable from SetHwReg8188EU() function in drivers/staging/r8188eu/hal/usb_halinit.c and also modifies the related code in rtw_p2p.c.

The commit changes a variable declaration from 'const' to 'static'. This change is made in the file 'arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c', where it declares an array of midr_range structs. The reason for this change is to prevent multiple definitions of the same symbol, which would lead to a linker error.

The commit removes an incorrect interrupt flags cell from the MDSS nodes in devicetree bindings for display/msm. The MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell, so specifying interrupt flags on the consumers is incorrect.

The commit message suggests that it is about allocating arrays in a struct ctrl_ref. The code changes involve adding space for arrays in the struct ctrl_ref and modifying how to handle dynamic array allocation.
The commit adds new PMICs (Power Management IC) models for different SoCs to the socinfo driver in the QCOM platform. These are specific to the SM8350 and SA8540P platforms.
The commit changes a switch-case statement in rtl8xxxu_core.c to recognize all possible chip cuts instead of printing 'unknown' when it's greater than E.
The commit removes a variable 'is_enabled' from the struct 'clk_prcmu'. This is done to reduce complexity in the code. The commit also modifies other parts of the code that use this variable.

The commit is about refactoring the code in 'arch/riscv/mm/init.c'. It moves a macro to an adjacent #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL and groups first type usage cases into one. The primary purpose seems to be improving the readability of the code by grouping related variables together.
The commit enables QUP (Quick Universal Peripheral) and GPI DMA hardware in a device tree file for the Sony Xperia Lena. This is done to add functioning I2C nodes later.

The commit fixes a typo in comments, improves code readability by ensuring that each comment starts on a new line, and replaces an unclear comment with one that better explains the purpose of a variable.

The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function of igc_i225.c file. The infinite loop occurs when the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired. This is typically caused by surprise removal of the hardware. In such cases, the code logs an error and quits without hanging indefinitely.

The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use more modern terminology for clocking. It changes a boolean variable name from 'master_mode' to 'provider_mode', and also modifies how this variable is used in different parts of the code.

The commit is removing a hack that was used to support future NFS swap functionality. The hack was checking if an inode was a swap file or not. However, the hack was unnecessary because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined and will always return false (0). Therefore, this commit fixes a bug.
The commit adds functionality to handle GTP echo requests in the Linux kernel's GTP driver. It also includes a fix for a bug where there was no userspace daemon handling GTP messages.
The commit replaces 'next' cpumask functions with 'first' ones in several files. This is done to improve the code efficiency by making it more effective and readable.

The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. This IOVAR is used to get TIM information from firmware which is no longer supported and hence this call will always fail. The failure results in an error message being displayed on the console all the time when roam is done.

The commit adds a new board configuration for the sof_rt5682 codec without an amplifier. It modifies two files: sof_rt5682.c and soc-acpi-intel-adl-match.c. The changes involve adding a new platform device ID for 'adl_rt5682' in the first file, and updating the matching machine configuration to include this new board.
The commit is adding a check to see if zerocopy sendmsg is supported by the socket before trying to use it. If not, it returns -EOPNOTSUPP.

The commit modifies a Sphinx documentation file to handle CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) characters in the kernel documentation. It checks if the Noto Sans CJK SC font is available and loads xeCJK package if it exists. This allows for better display of Asian languages in the documentation.

The commit modifies a script named checkpatch.pl in the scripts directory. This script is used to check patches for Linux kernel development. The modification adds an exception to the case macro detection in the script. It does not fix any bugs or add new features.

The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in the drm/simpledrm driver. The primary purpose of this change is to improve the architecture and code sharing among drivers. It does not fix any bugs or add new features.

The commit adds a new interface for jpeg decoding worker in MT8195. It also introduces three functions to manage the hardware resources of the decoder and one function to handle the timeout of the decoding process.

The commit removes pointless VPRINTK() calls from the codebase. These are debugging statements that print information to the kernel log when certain conditions are met. The purpose of these calls is to provide additional context about what's happening in the system, but they don't affect the functionality or behaviour of the system.

The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in the AMD display driver. This involves modifying the code to handle extra vblank lines gained from freesync and entering zstate if possible.
The commit updates a function pointer prototype in kgd_kfd_interface.h to match reality so that there is no more CFI violation.

The commit corrects a typo in the 'stm32-pinctrl.yaml' file. It changes the property name from 'reset' to 'resets'. This is done to ensure that the binding is correctly used and does not cause any errors.
The commit is about silencing a GCC warning related to array bounds in the ath9k_hw_resettxqueue function. The warning is about an out-of-bounds access of the 'txq' array.

The commit changes the power management code in sun8i-ss driver to use pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync. This change is intended to simplify the code and improve its readability.

The commit is about splitting out fixed file installation and removal from the io_uring/filetable.c file into a separate function. The new function takes an io_ring_ctx pointer instead of an io_kiocb pointer as its first argument.

The commit updates the device tree bindings for NXP's IMX8MQ VPU. It splits the original binding into two separate ones, one for each decoder block (G1 and G2). The new bindings include power-domain support, which is a perfective change.
The commit removes a macro named IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON from the iwlwifi driver's configuration file. This removal is likely part of a refactoring effort to simplify and improve code readability.
